channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Inappropriate tree planting planned on Melbourne Grove SE22 8SA, 1st April
item.description:
I understand there is a plan for a tree to be planted between 141and 143 Melbourne Grove and a second tree to be planted on the corner near 145 Melbourne Grove. The pavements here are narrow and the houses front directly onto the pavement, so the trunk of the tree would be less than 1.5 mitres from the front walls. To add further complications these houses have basements that extends to the limit of houses’ front wall, so root ingress is a potential problem. In addition buildings insurance becomes a major issue for our properties. Furthermore there is water pump drainage system in the roadway which is powered at 145 Melbourne Grove, (details available from Thames Water) which makes the whole stretch of pavement unsuitable for trees. Previously the tree planting service in Southwalk had agreed that this section of pavement was unsuitable for tree planting following our earlier objections. For these reasons please register my objection the the proposed plantings.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 143, Melbourne Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over hanging hedge reduces pavement, 1st April
item.description:
Hedge has been allowed to grow over the footpath and needs cutting back to boundary.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 42, Tudor Way, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hedge blocking pavement, 1st April
item.description:
Hedge has been allowed to grow over the footpath and needs cutting back to boundary.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tudor Way, Poverest, Greater London

item.title: Road sign fallen from pavement, 1st April
item.description:
A road sign has fallen from the pavement, breaking the paving stones and creating a trip hazard. Please repair urgently before it either damages a car or a person.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Finchley Road, Golders Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped cardboard, 1st April
item.description:
Dumped cardboard

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stockwell Green, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street cleaning missed on Southwark side of Plough Way, 1st April
item.description:
The Southwark side of Plough Way has not been cleaned for more than 2 months and currently has a significant amount of litter along the curb, especially between parked cars (see attached photo). The opposite side of the street was cleaned last Friday, likely as it falls under a different council, but this side appears to have been missed. Please could this side be cleaned as well?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Plough Way, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Business has shutdown and left a bench on pavement, 1st April
item.description:
A business has shutdown and relocated but has not taken a bench that they have left bolted into the public pavement. This is causing anti-social behaviour with people using it to sit and drink publicly in the late evenings and early mornings.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rivington Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Piles of dog excrement, 1st April
item.description:
This is disgusting and needs to be removed. Recent problems with this in the Albert Barnes House front garden also.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): New Kent Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Littering on Anerley Park Road - marked as 'Fixed' but not solved! Please resolve!, 1st April
item.description:
Previous littering case was marked as fixed- there has been no change to the littering on the street. PLEASE DO NOT MARK AS FIXED UNTIL CLEANING HAS TAKEN PLACE! See photos enclosed, the length of the street is covered in empty beer tins, crisp packets, discarded cardboard boxes - needs to be swept.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4, Anerley Park Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Junction Carlisle rd and Dunmore rd, 1st April
item.description:
Stuff dumped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Carlisle Road, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: No street lighting, 1st April
item.description:

item.title: Opposite No 1 Dunmore Rd, 1st April
item.description:
Electrical item dumped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dunmore Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Tree planting scheduled for inappropriate location., 1st April
item.description:
We have been made aware that two trees are scheduled to be planted outside and near our home. One is directly outside our house on the boundary between us and our neighbour at No. 141 and the other on the corner adjacent to No 145. The tree at 141/143 is in a location where a previous tree had to be removed after causing structural damage to our neighbours @ No 141’s property. They have been told by their insurers that a tree being planted will invalidate their insurance. This terrace of houses have an unusual below ground drainage network relying on a pump maintained by Thames Water outside No. 145. The tree proposed on the corner is close to this and we would be concerned that this could damage the pump on which we all rely. Whilst it would be great to have trees outside our house and is something we would normally welcome and support. This section of Melbourne Grove with narrow pavements and no front gardens is a wholly inappropriate location and should not be considered. In addition to this, since we have no choice but to keep our bins on this narrow pavement, the inclusion of a tree would further limit the pavement from an accessibility perspective.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 141, Melbourne Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There is a wild space that crosses deed boundaries that is being destroyed by surrounding residents., 1st April
item.description:
There is a wild space that has been registered as a wildlife space that I have managed for10 years. However, healthy brambles an elderberry tree and a silver birch tree that was within my boundary have been severely reduced or in the case of my healthy silver birch, destroyed. I would like to claim ownership of this nature reserve space to enable it to provide safe haven for wildlife without risk of pesticide use or being destroyed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Brindley Street, New Cross, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Craters like the Moon - NASA ERROR POSSIBLE, 1st April
item.description:
As this area has STILL not been repaired, in the coming days there is a real risk that the NASA Spaceship ARTEMIS || may mistake the top of St Pauls Wood Hill for the Moon and this will not please the Americans! - Get it fixed eh !
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leesons Hill, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead tree - reference Report ref: 7834316., 1st April
item.description:
Just received update from my enquiry of 2025 saying case resolved - BUT no explanation or solution offered by Bexley Council. Please can you confirm if you are replacing the tree ? As I made the donation and paid for the tree in the first place so expect a bit more than "Resolved"? Is it dead or not? Are you replacing it? Can I take cost out of the increased council tax?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 122, Hurst Road, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pile of rubbish on Truman’s Road, 1st April
item.description:
As is a daily occurrence, there is litter all over Truman’s Road but what looks like a pile of recycling is on the pavement, likely dropped by the bin men and not picked up.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salcombe Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping on Truman’s Road, 1st April
item.description:
Fly tipping in two different area on the same side of the road on Truman’s Road - there’s a chair and then in the direction of Stoke Newington Road there is a shower screen, plastic and cardboard behind the telecom cabinet. It happens every day.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salcombe Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fridge outside my shed, 1st April
item.description:
It’s been abandoned for about a month & has been reported twice…..I cannot get access to my shed because of this item being dumped outside of it

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nile Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Leak or Blocked Drain, 1st April
item.description:
This water has been here for a while now and does not drain away. Not sure if it is a leak or the drain is blocked.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 67, Oaklands Avenue, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
